title: Chart Widget to show Zero/Empty Values - All Column Types
excerpt:
I’m looking to find a way to show zero/empty values in the chart widget created in dashboards and/or Docs.

posts.author: Jaki (Jaclyn)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m looking to find a way to show zero/empty values in the chart widget created in dashboards and/or Docs.

For background, we often use WorkForms via monday.com to survey stakeholders and the data collected aggregates over time. Data collected is used for broader reporting - we are currently trialing the use of Dashboards and Docs to both collate and present findings to internal/external stakeholders.

The issue is that when we create Dashboards etc to facilitate reporting, we are unable to illustrate any empty values (especially for dropdown, or select fields/columns).

For example, I have a status column (single select field) that has 5 possible options available. Only 3 have been selected based on forms submitted. When I create a chart/graph I can only see the 3 selected options populated (even with the “show empty values” box ticked in “customise settings” menu).

This a really problematic for us as options that have not been selected are valuable data points in themselves.

The only workaround currently is to export data via excel and recreate the graphs there which leads to a huge duplication of work and makes the feasibility of using Dashboards and/or Docs for client reporting less attractive.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great suggestion - have you explored if any of the reporting apps can do this yet Jaki?

posts.author: MMatharu (Megan Matharu)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree with this. We use dashboards for reporting and have a status column with options that are currently not in use, as they are temporary statuses (valid for a few weeks). The chart should show all options of the status otherwise, leadership ask why it’s been ommited from the report. Why is there a “show empty values” within the “customise” section if it doesn’t work?

image409×128 9.46 KB

posts.author: SMower (Seth Mower)
posts.cookedHtml: My agency uses forms and charts all the time where we specifically want to see where metrics are 0, which currently impossible unless we go back and manually enter that data. I’d love to see this feature, it would be incredibly useful for us.
